ALL SPECIALTIES / CERTIFIED NURSING ASSISTANT / NORTH DAKOTA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

North Dakota Certified Nursing Assistant Certification

North Dakota Certified Nursing Assistant license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-22 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Certified Nursing Assistant applications in North Dakota run through North Dakota Board of Nursing. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Proof of Training - Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
  • State Competency Exam Results - Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 2 to 4 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

  1. STEP 1

    Create NDBON Online Portal Account

    Register for a new user account on the NDBON Online Portal (ndbon.org/Portal) using your legal name and email address.

    Fee: $25 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Complete Online Application

    Log in to your NDBON Online Portal account, select the Nursing Assistant Application, and complete all required sections, including personal information and education/training details.

    Fee: $25 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Submit Required Documents & Fees

    Upload any necessary documentation (e.g., proof of training, identification) directly through the portal and pay the non-refundable application fee of $40.

    Fee: $25 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Register and Pass NNAAP Exam

    Register for the National Nurse Aide Assessment Program (NNAAP) exam through Prometric, complete both the written/oral and skills components, and achieve a passing score.

    Fee: $25 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Await Registry Listing

    Prometric will send your exam results to the NDBON. The Board will then review your application and exam results for compliance with state requirements.

    Fee: $25 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Verify Listing Status

    Monitor your application status via your NDBON Online Portal account and confirm your listing on the North Dakota Nursing Assistant Registry once approved.

    Fee: $25 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$25
Background check
Varies by provider
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$25

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
North Dakota Board of Nursing
Phone
(701) 328-9777
Website
ndbon.org/
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Certified Nursing Assistant renewal in North Dakota follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$20
Continuing education
12 hours per renewal cycle

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does North Dakota CNA endorsement cost?

The endorsement application fee is $25. Additional costs may include background check fees.

Is a background check required?

Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in North Dakota.

How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to North Dakota?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

Can I start working in North Dakota while waiting?

Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$25

Processing time

2 to 4 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

NNAAP (Prometric)

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years · $20

Licensing board

North Dakota Board of Nursing

ndbon.org/

(701) 328-9777
